description: Tests the toroidal geometry hypothesis for the Laporta constants from three directions: (1) beta-content classification using PSLQ null results confirms all six are beta^0 (no pi content), (2) elliptic magnitude scan checks whether |C_i| matches rational multiples of K(k) or E(k) at any modulus, (3) ratio analysis checks whether inter-integral ratios match simple geometric numbers.
thesis: The Laporta integrals are beta^0 (no spherical angular content) but may carry toroidal angular content expressible as elliptic periods. Their magnitudes and ratios should constrain the moduli of the underlying elliptic curves.
formula: C_i =? (p/q) * K(k_topology) or (p/q) * K(k)^a * E(k)^b for rational p/q and specific modulus k
